test_id_1.query: Compare Medicaid versus provider manual guidance for dual eligible pharmacy claims.
test_id_1.answer:
{
  "headline_summary": "According to the newest policy source dated 2023-04-01, dual eligible pharmacy claims are handled by NYRx for Dual Eligible NYS Medicaid members who are enrolled in a Medicare Part D drug plan or Medicare Advantage Drug Contracting (MAPD) plan. The provider manual guidance from the New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines dated 2025-10-01 states that all prescription drugs are reimbursed on a fee-for-service basis.",
  "policy_definition": "[Document Title: Medicaid Update Special Edition Part Three: NYRx Pharmacy Benefit Transition Volume 39 Number 4] — [Chunk ID: mu_no4_mar23_speced_pr.pdf::0003] — 2023-03-01 — [pages: [2, 3]]",

  "differences": ["Difference: The provider manual states that the total Medicare/Medicaid payment to the pharmacy provider will not exceed the amount that the pharmacy provider would have received for a Medicaid-only patient. This is not explicitly stated in the policy source.", "[Document Title: New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines] — [Chunk ID: Pharmacy_Policy_Guidelines.pdf::0125] — 2025-10-01 — [pages: [30, 31]]"],

}
